name: New Zealand
summary: Strong settlement fit for a Bangladeshi dual-software-engineer couple: Software Engineer is Green List Tier 1 (straight-to-residence), master's grads get up to 3y open post-study work, the partner of a level 9 student works freely, and citizenship comes in ~5y with dual allowed. Main frictions are no NZ mission in Dhaka with only ~45% Bangladeshi student-visa success, high CS tuition, and a smaller/lower-paying tech market than Australia or the US.

links.title: Immigration New Zealand
INZ news centre

job-market.summary: Software Engineer on Green List Tier 1; AI/data/cyber demand rising, but small isolated market with pay below AU/US.
job-market.reasoning: Both spouses are software engineers, an occupation INZ classes as a Green List Tier 1 skill shortage (ANZSCO 261313), so sponsorship is genuinely accessible and AI/data/cyber demand is rising. However, New Zealand is a geographically isolated, small market with fewer large employers; average SE total compensation is about NZD 131,000 (range ~NZD 94,000-161,000), clearly below Australian and US levels, so it scores decent-to-strong rather than best-in-class.
evidence: Software Engineer (261313) and other ICT roles (Developer/Analyst Programmer, ICT Security Specialist) sit on Green List Tier 1.
Average SE total compensation ~NZD 131,000; range ~NZD 94,000-161,000; senior Auckland ~NZD 143,500 (2026).
Salaries materially below AU/US; few large employers, remote-first hiring compresses mid-tier rates.
Immigration median wage rises to NZD 35.00/hr from 9 Mar 2026 (Green List pay reference).

links.title: Green List Tier 1 ICT roles (NZ Shores)
INZ - new NOL occupations and median wage increase (9 Mar 2026)

visa-access.summary: No NZ mission in Dhaka but decided online by INZ (no New Delhi travel); biometrics at VFS Dhaka; only ~45% BD student success.
visa-access.reasoning: There is no New Zealand mission in Dhaka, but Bangladeshi nationals apply online to INZ with biometrics captured at VFS Global Dhaka, so no New Delhi or third-country travel is required - a genuine advantage over many destinations. The drag is a reported ~45% Bangladeshi student-visa success rate in 2025-26, a compulsory Genuine Student statement, and maintenance funds of roughly BDT 60-70 lakh held 5-6 months beforehand, which together form a real credibility and financial bar.
evidence: No Dhaka visa office; applications lodged online with INZ, decisions issued electronically (no New Delhi travel).
Biometrics (fingerprints/photo) collected at VFS Global Dhaka after submission.
Reported Bangladeshi student-visa approval ~45% in 2026 (varies by institution and application quality).
Funds ~BDT 60-70 lakh held 5-6 months pre-application; police clearance and INZ panel-physician medical required.
Processing typically 3-7 weeks, longer in peak periods.

links.title: Study and student visas (INZ)
NZ student visa from Bangladesh - requirements & ~45% ratio

citizenship.summary: Citizenship after ~5y residence (1,350 days, 240/yr), dual allowed; new civics test lands H2 2027.
citizenship.reasoning: Citizenship by grant needs a resident visa held for 5 years with at least 1,350 days present (minimum 240 days per 12-month period), matching the brief's ~5yr-plus-dual anchor. New Zealand permits dual citizenship and Bangladesh allows it too, so the couple keep their Bangladeshi passports - a meaningful plus. From the second half of 2027 a mandatory 20-question civics test (75% pass) will apply to most adult applicants, adding modest future friction.
evidence: By grant: 5 years as a resident; 1,350 days total presence and 240 days minimum per 12-month period.
Dual citizenship explicitly allowed by New Zealand (and by Bangladesh).
English assessed informally (basic conversation), not by a fixed test - suits fluent IT professionals.
From H2 2027: mandatory civics test, 20 questions, 75% pass, ages 16-65.

links.title: NZ citizenship presence requirements (govt.nz)
New civics test for citizenship by grant (1News, May 2026)

post-study-work.summary: Master's grads get up to 3y open Post Study Work Visa; unrestricted employer/role; eligibility expanding 16 Nov 2026.
post-study-work.reasoning: A master's (Level 9) completed with at least 30 weeks full-time study qualifies for an open Post Study Work Visa of up to 3 years, letting both spouses work for any employer anywhere and then switch to an AEWV or Green List residence. From 16 November 2026 eligibility expands further (Level 7 graduate diplomas plus a bachelor's degree), and PSWV holders can sponsor partners and dependent children, so this is best-in-class for the couple.
evidence: Master's/doctoral grads get the full up-to-3-year open PSWV (Level 7+ needs 30 weeks full-time study).
Open conditions: any work, any job, anywhere in New Zealand.
From 16 Nov 2026: PSWV extended to Level 7 grad-diploma holders who also hold a bachelor's degree.
PSWV holders can support partners/children for visitor, work or dependent student visas.
Both engineers can each independently hold their own PSWV.

links.title: Post Study Work Visa (INZ)
NZ expands post-study work options from 16 Nov 2026 (ICEF Monitor)

spouse-family.summary: Partner of a level 9 (master's) student gets an open work visa regardless of field; both engineers can each hold own visa.
spouse-family.reasoning: Because both spouses are software engineers, neither depends on the other - each can hold their own student, PSW or Green List visa. Even in the study phase the partner of a Level 9/10 (master's/PhD) student can get an open work visa irrespective of subject, and partners of AEWV holders in skilled roles also get open work rights, giving full spouse employment throughout study and work phases. This matches the brief's top anchor of both working freely throughout.
evidence: Partner of a Level 9/10 student gets an open work visa, no field-of-study restriction (NZD 4,200 savings, genuine-relationship test).
Partner-of-student work visa moved to INZ's enhanced Immigration Online system on 1 Jun 2026.
Partners of AEWV holders in skilled roles get open work rights (wage thresholds apply).
Both spouses are engineers, so each can independently hold student/PSW/Green List status.
Dependent children can accompany on visitor/student visas.

links.title: Partner of a Student Work Visa (INZ)
Post Study Work Visa - support partner/children (INZ)

msc-study.summary: English-native CS MSc widely available, partner accompanies & works, 25h/wk study work; but tuition ~NZD 32k-54k/yr.
msc-study.reasoning: New Zealand universities teach master's degrees in English with no language transition, students may work up to 25 hours/week in semester, and the partner can accompany and work - a strong combination for this couple. The drawback is cost: international MS Computer Science tuition runs roughly NZD 32,000-54,000/year plus about NZD 20,000-26,000/year living, and broad scholarships for self-funded Bangladeshi applicants are scarce, keeping it mid-band.
evidence: MS Computer Science tuition ~NZD 32,000-54,000/year for international students (STEM at higher end).
Living costs ~NZD 20,000-26,000/year; INZ maintenance proof NZD 20,000/year.
Eligible students may work up to 25 hours/week in semester (raised in 2025).
Partner of a master's student can accompany and work; broad funded scholarships scarce for self-funded BD applicants.

links.title: Study with New Zealand (govt)
MS in Computer Science NZ - 2026 fees (LeapScholar)

pr-pathway.summary: Green List Tier 1 Software Engineer = Straight to Residence; apply for residence immediately on a qualifying job offer.
pr-pathway.reasoning: Software Engineer is a Green List Tier 1 role, a Straight to Residence pathway: with a qualifying Accredited Employer job offer meeting the wage requirement (median NZD 35.00/hr from 9 Mar 2026), the couple can apply for a resident visa immediately, with no minimum NZ work period - among the fastest, clearest routes in the set. The 6-point Skilled Migrant Category is an alternative, and August 2026 SMC changes cut required NZ work experience to two years and remove the wage step-up at application.
evidence: Software Engineer = Green List Tier 1 (Straight to Residence), no set NZ work period before applying.
Tier 2 'Work to Residence' roles need 24 months work; Tier 1 does not.
Aug 2026 SMC changes: NZ work experience cut from 3 to 2 years; no wage increase needed at application.
Some specialist IT roles require pay above the median wage to qualify.

links.title: Green List Tier 1 / Straight to Residence (NZ Shores)
Further SMC changes from August 2026 (INZ)

income-cost.summary: Decent SE pay (~NZD 94k-161k) but high Auckland rent and tech salaries below AU/US trim net savings.
income-cost.reasoning: Software engineer pay of roughly NZD 94,000-161,000 (avg total comp ~NZD 131,000) supports a dual-income couple and beats most of Europe net of tax. However, Auckland living costs run about NZD 1,700-2,200/month excluding rent, NZ tech pay sits clearly below Australian and US levels, and purchasing power favours Australia, so net savings are moderate rather than strong - a mixed-to-decent result.
evidence: SE total comp avg ~NZD 131,000; range ~NZD 94,000-161,000; entry-level lower.
Auckland living ~NZD 1,700-2,200/month excluding rent; rent charged weekly.
NZ tech pay below AU/US; purchasing power favours Australia.
Dual software income materially improves household savings capacity.

links.title: Software Engineer salary NZ (PayScale)
Cost of living Auckland 2026

healthcare.summary: Universal publicly funded healthcare for residents and 2+ year work-visa holders; students need private insurance.
healthcare.reasoning: Once on a resident visa or a work visa allowing a continuous stay of two years or more (counted from first arrival), the couple access New Zealand's publicly funded universal healthcare with subsidised GP visits and prescriptions - a clear quality and cost advantage. During the study phase they must hold approved private student health insurance, a minor cost, which keeps the score just below top tier.
evidence: Residents and work-visa holders staying 2+ years get publicly funded health services (Health NZ Te Whatu Ora).
International students must show health/travel insurance for a Fee-paying Student Visa.
Subsidised GP visits and prescriptions for eligible residents.
High-quality public hospital system.

links.title: Who can get public health care (INZ)
Guide to eligibility for public health services (Te Whatu Ora)

culture-language.summary: English-native country; no language barrier for fluent IT professionals; multicultural and open - very favourable.
culture-language.reasoning: New Zealand is English-speaking across study, work and daily life, so two English-fluent software engineers face essentially no language barrier, and citizenship needs only basic conversational English. The multicultural, migrant-heavy population (especially in Auckland) eases settlement; a small notch off the top reflects geographic isolation rather than any integration barrier.
evidence: English is the primary working and academic language.
Citizenship requires only basic conversational English (no fixed test until the 2027 civics test).
Highly multicultural Auckland with large migrant communities.
IELTS/PTE used only for study/visa entry, not daily integration.

links.title: Live in New Zealand (INZ)
New civics test for citizenship by grant (1News, May 2026)

safety-law.summary: Top-tier rule of law (WJP 2025 #5 of 143 globally, score ~0.83), low crime, strong rights protections.
safety-law.reasoning: New Zealand ranks 5th of 143 jurisdictions in the 2025 WJP Rule of Law Index with an overall score around 0.83, sitting in the global top five alongside Denmark, Norway, Finland and Sweden, and its score edged up year on year. Low crime, strong fundamental-rights protection and stable institutions make it a very safe, fair destination for the couple.
evidence: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025: 5th of 143 globally; top-5 with the Nordics; score ~0.83.
Among the few countries whose overall score rose (<1%) in 2025.
Top performer in the East Asia & Pacific region.
Generally low violent-crime rates and fair treatment of migrants.

links.title: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 - global press release
WJP Rule of Law Index - New Zealand

politics.summary: Stable democracy; 2025-26 reforms actively court skilled migrants (NOL expansion, AEWV easing, PSWV growth).
politics.reasoning: New Zealand is a stable parliamentary democracy, and 2025-26 policy is actively migrant-friendly: the National Occupation List replaced ANZSCO from 3 Nov 2025 (87 new occupations, +47 from 9 Mar 2026), the AEWV median-wage threshold was removed and required experience cut from three to two years, post-study work options expand on 16 Nov 2026, and SMC changes land Aug 2026. This steady pro-skilled-migration stance directly benefits the couple.
evidence: From 3 Nov 2025 the National Occupation List replaced ANZSCO; 87 new occupations, +47 from 9 Mar 2026.
AEWV median-wage threshold removed; required experience cut from 3 to 2 years.
PSWV eligibility expands 16 Nov 2026; SMC liberalisation from Aug 2026.
Stable institutions and orderly democratic transitions.

links.title: Recognising NOL occupations in the AEWV (INZ)
INZ news centre

tax.summary: No general CGT or wealth tax, no separate social-security tax; top rate 39%, GST 15%, ACC levy 1.67%.
tax.reasoning: New Zealand has no general capital gains tax, no wealth tax and no separate social-security payroll tax, only a 1.67% ACC earner's levy (capped at ~NZD 2,552), keeping the effective burden on two software salaries moderate. The top marginal rate of 39% applies only above NZD 180,000 for 2025-26, with GST at 15%.
evidence: No general capital gains tax and no wealth tax.
No dedicated social-security tax; ACC earner's levy 1.67% (max ~NZD 2,552).
GST 15%; KiwiSaver contributions optional.

links.title: IRD - tax rates for individuals
NZ tax brackets 2025-2026

muslim-diaspora.summary: ~57k-60k Muslims, mosques and halal in Auckland, but small Bangladeshi community (~3,550); strong post-2019 solidarity.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: New Zealand's Muslim population is about 57,000-60,000 (roughly 1.1%) across 40+ ethnicities, with numerous Auckland mosques (Ponsonby, Lynfield, Al Maqtoum) and widely available halal food, so practising is feasible. However, the Bangladeshi community is small (around 3,550 in the 2023 census), placing it at the brief's 'present but small' anchor; the 2019 Christchurch attack remains context but was followed by strong national solidarity and protective measures.
evidence: Muslim population ~57,000-60,000 (~1.1%); South Asians a major share of 40+ ethnicities.
Bangladeshi-born community ~3,550 (2023 census), concentrated in Auckland - small.
Numerous Auckland mosques and Islamic centres; halal food widely available.
Post-2019 Christchurch: strong solidarity, protective legislation and community support.
